role_description: Safety officer working on industrial construction or refinery projects. Responsible for monitoring field safety compliance, conducting inspections and audits, enforcing PPE requirements, reviewing work permits, investigating incidents and near-misses, leading toolbox talks, and ensuring all work follows OSHA, client, and company safety standards.
report_focus: When structuring this person's reports, look for: safety observations (positive and negative), near-misses or incidents, permit compliance, PPE violations, housekeeping conditions, hazard identifications, corrective actions taken, safety meetings conducted, areas inspected, stop-work authority exercised, and trending safety concerns.
safety_rules: All personnel must wear required PPE at all times: hard hat, safety glasses, steel-toe boots, FR clothing
Report all incidents, near-misses, and unsafe conditions immediately to your supervisor
Never work on energized equipment without proper LOTO (Lockout/Tagout) procedures
Confined space entry requires a permit, atmospheric testing, and a dedicated attendant
Hot work requires a permit and a fire watch for minimum 30 minutes after work completion
Fall protection is required at heights of 6 feet or more — 100% tie-off at all times
Keep work areas clean and organized — good housekeeping prevents injuries
Know the location of the nearest fire extinguisher, eye wash station, and first aid kit
Never bypass or remove safety guards, barriers, or warning signs
All scaffolds must be inspected and tagged before use — never use a red-tagged scaffold
Attend daily toolbox talks and JSA reviews before starting work
Report any damaged tools or equipment immediately — do not use defective equipment
Maintain 3 points of contact when climbing ladders
Be aware of overhead hazards and never stand under suspended loads
Excavations deeper than 4 feet require shoring, sloping, or a trench box
All workers must complete site orientation before starting work
Drug and alcohol policies are strictly enforced — fit for duty at all times
Emergency evacuation routes must be known — know your muster point
Never operate equipment you are not trained and authorized to use
Ground Fault Circuit Interrupters (GFCI) required for all temporary power
Document all safety observations — both positive and negative
Stop work authority must be exercised when imminent danger is identified
Incident investigations must identify root causes, not just symptoms
Safety metrics must be tracked daily: observations, near-misses, first aids, recordables
All permits must be reviewed and signed before work begins
Conduct daily area inspections of all active work zones
Verify all workers have completed required training and orientation
Monitor weather conditions and implement heat/cold stress protocols as needed
Ensure proper barricading and signage at all hazardous work areas
Review and approve all JSAs and pre-task plans before work starts
tools_and_equipment: Inspect all tools before each use — remove damaged tools from service
Power tools must have guards in place and be properly grounded
Pneumatic tools require whip checks on all hose connections
Grinding wheels must be properly dressed and have tongue guards
Eye protection required when using any striking or cutting tools
Portable ladders must extend 3 feet above landing surface
Extension cords must be rated for the load and inspected daily
Lifting equipment (chain hoists, come-alongs) must have current inspection tags
Welding leads must be properly insulated — no exposed conductors
Hydraulic jacks and presses must not exceed rated capacity
Battery-powered tools must use manufacturer-approved batteries
Scaffolds require toe boards, mid-rails, and top rails at 42 inches
All rigging hardware must be inspected before each lift
Torque wrenches must have current calibration stickers
Temporary lighting must be properly guarded and rated for the area classification
language_notes: Safety officers may report in English or Spanish. Safety-critical terms should be preserved exactly as spoken. Common Spanish safety terms: 'casco' (hard hat), 'lentes' (safety glasses), 'guantes' (gloves), 'arnes' (harness), 'permiso' (permit), 'peligro' (danger), 'incidente' (incident), 'riesgo' (risk/hazard).
